calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-02-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Second Renewed Motion And Second Renewed Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JEFFREY LEE's Motion And Second Renewed Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action.

Plaintiff Jeffrey Lee's unopposed Second Renewed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, the court orders the case dismissed as follows: (1) Plaintiff's representative PAGA claims alleged to have occurred from April 14, 2021, to December 16, 2024, (which is time period included in the Minas Settlement) are dismissed with prejudice; (3) Plaintiff's representative PAGA claims alleged that fall outside of the Minas PAGA Period are dismissed without prejudice; and, (3) Plaintiff's individual PAGA claims in full are dismissed with prejudice.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Renewed Notice And Renewed Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action
Off Calendar Apr-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JEFFREY LEE's Notice And Renewed Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action.

Plaintiff Jeffrey Lee's unopposed Renewed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action is OFF CALENDAR.

On February 20, 2025, and by way of its adoption of its tentative ruling from the previous afternoon, the court ordered Plaintiff to prepare a proposed order that includes the full text of the court's tentative ruling denying without prejudice Plaintiff's original motion to dismiss the representative action. More than seven weeks have passed, and Plaintiff has not yet submitted the proposed order. Plaintiff may re-notice its renewed motion after he has complied with the court's February 20, 2025 order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-17-2025 Continued To May-21-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice And Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action
Off Calendar Feb-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JEFFREY LEE's Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action.

Plaintiff Jeffrey Lee's Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The motion is infirm in several respects. First, Plaintiff did not submit proof of service on LWDA. Second, the motion itself requests dismissal with prejudice of all representative claims, whereas the Lu Declaration seeks dismissal with prejudice of some representative claims and dismissal without prejudice of others. Third, absent is a declaration under penalty of perjury identifying the similarities and differences between the claims in the instant action and those in Minas v. Panda Express Inc. L.A.S.C. 22STCV077753, and explaining with some particularity why dismissal of the representative claims in this action is appropriate. Fourth, the motion does not explain why Plaintiff seeks dismissal instead of, for example, leave to amend to limit the claims in this action to those not covered in the Minas action. The court understands that this action includes claims, even if identical, that cover a period earlier than the period covered by Minas. The court is concerned that if those claims are dismissed, even without prejudice, they will be forfeited entirely because a subsequent action would be untimely. Any party making a substantially similar motion in this case in the future shall include the text of this ruling in its papers.

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order that includes the above text and drop the hard copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
